# EvalRig — Independent AI Benchmarks

> EvalRig tests LLM outputs against real enterprise tasks using deterministic rules and ensemble LLM judges.

## What This Site Contains

- Independent benchmark results comparing frontier AI models (Claude, GPT, Gemini)
- Skill-by-skill scoring breakdowns with pass/fail thresholds
- Cost analysis per model per task
- Methodology: 60% deterministic rule scoring + 40% ensemble judge (3 frontier models, median aggregation, provider recusal)

## Benchmarks

- [Three Frontier Models, Five Agentic Coding Skills](https://evalrig.ai/benchmarks/frontier-3way/): Claude Opus 4.7 vs GPT-5.4 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro on 5 agentic coding skills with iterative test execution. All three pass every skill at 100%. Opus highest quality (0.979, $10.33), Gemini best value (0.952, $2.98), GPT-5.4 in the middle (0.934, $5.24 — uses 2-3× more tool rounds on concurrency work).
- [Customer Success Doesn't Need Opus](https://evalrig.ai/benchmarks/cs-department/): Nine models across three tiers on four CS tasks. Gemini 3.1 Pro won (0.852). GPT-4.1 Mini best value ($0.45/quality point). Opus came last.

## Key Facts

- Pass threshold: 0.70
- Temperature: 0.0 (deterministic)
- Ensemble judge uses provider recusal (no model grades its own provider)
- Scoring: median across 3 independent judges
- All benchmarks run with EvalRig CLI tool

## About

Built by Mads Srinivasan at Turanu AI.
Website: https://evalrig.ai
Blog: https://turanu.ai
